title: cyathea pholidota
intro: Cyathea pholidota, also known as the 'leather fern', is a species of fern native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. The leaves and stems of Cyathea pholidota can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
